IX. 1. prepared for the righteous. avert their eyes from iniquity. Ps. cxix. 37. make righteous judgement. Ezek. xviii. 8.
IX. 1. prepared for the righteous. avert their eyes from iniquity. Ps. cxix. 37. make righteous judgement. Ezek. xviii. 8.
To the chief Musician, A Psalm of David the servant of the Lord. JHE wicked, who live in transgres-
To the chief Musician, A Psalm of David the servant of the Lord. JHE wicked, who live in transgres-
Therefore, too, “in thy light we shall see light”-in that light which is treasured for the righteous in the world to come, and with which Israel will be illuminated.
Therefore, too, “in thy light we shall see light”-in that light which is treasured for the righteous in the world to come, and with which Israel will be illuminated.

Thus, through the tzaddik/the ruach, haughtiness is eliminated, as explained. As is written (Psalms 36:12), “Let not the foot of pride come against me.” Idolatry is also eliminated. As is written (Genesis 18:4), “… and wash your feet”—this alludes to idolatry (Bava Metzia 86b).
